summary: Molecular subtyping of low-grade cerebellar astrocytomas provides improved diagnostic clarity but does not significantly impact patient management decisions such as monitoring schedules or therapeutic interventions due to limited differences in outcome prediction across subclasses. Thus, its clinical relevance remains minimal despite advances in genomic characterization.
